high energy phosphate bonds

Cellular respiration – the big picture

Oxidation-Reduction or “Redox” reaction
X e - +  Y    X  +  Y e -
oxidation
reduction
e - (electron) has a negative charge
picking up an electron lowers or reduces the charge
many molecules don’t want net negative charge
pick up H + as well to stay neutral

Cellular Respiration – summary reaction
C 6 H 12 O 6 + O 2 CO 2 +   H 2 O
oxidation
reduction
Oxygen is an electron hog
takes electrons away = electron acceptor
“oxidizes” molecules
Oxygen picks up electrons, becomes reduced
picks up H + too – stays neutral
forms H 2 O
C 6 H 12 O 6 + 6 O 2 6 CO 2 +  6 H 2 O

Pyruvate
The Krebs Cycle
aka “Citric Acid Cycle”
aka “TCA Cycle”
finish breaking down glucose
into CO 2 molecules

The Krebs Cycle
it’s a CYCLE
re-use carbon backbone
give off CO 2 as waste
pass e - to carriers
generate NADH
and FADH 2
make (a little) ATP
Your book’s version – you don’t need this much detail

Krebs Cycle summary
Inputs
acetyl-CoA
3 NAD +
FAD
ADP
Outputs
CoA
3 NADH energy
FADH 2 products
ATP
2 CO 2 waste product
From 2 pyruvates – products of 1 glucose molecule:
6 NADH
2 FADH 2
2 ATP
4 CO 2
